Software horror: litellm PyPI supply chain attack. 

Simple `pip install litellm` was enough to exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, env vars (all your API keys), shell history, crypto wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD secrets, database

Action Text now officially supports different editors. The adapter for Lexxy will be available in the next version: 
https://
github.com/basecamp/lexxy
/pull/778
…

Hopefully, other editors will embrace this. Rails is multi-editor now !

Kudos to Sean Doyle for doing the bulk of the work here.

Fuck it, we just made Fizzy completely free.

The open source installable version was always free, but the SaaS version was pay. No more. Basecamp and HEY's largess will subsidize Fizzy for all.

So go grab your account at 
http://
Fizzy.do. It's Kanban the way it should
Fizzy is now fully free! Just another gift from us to you. This is an excellent setup for working with agents too. We'll beef up the CLI shortly to make it even better. Enjoy!

Tomorrow needs to be tended to by planting new seeds. New companies. New growth. New capital. And that's just not going to happen if the Danish state declares itself at war with capital formation or accumulation.

Panther Lake is a real turning point for Intel. I've been on AMD since switching to Linux, but with 18A delivering Gelsinger's dream from 2021, it's a privilege to run blue again on the laptop side. And Intel working directly to get the most out of it on Omarchy is just .

This isn't just a win for Intel, but for America too. Having leading-edge CPUs produced in Arizona is a huge achievement. Next step is GPUs. Then other customers. Then full American sovereignty over its chip production.

You don't get Silicon Valley without the wealth accumulation! You need wealthy individuals to act as angels and investors. The Danish state can do many things well, but allocate capital is not one of them. This is evidenced by the near total death of new, large companies there.

AI coding agents can now deliver one-shot custom apps straight to your phone. 

It’s the beginning of the end for the iPhone’s dominance.
A lot of software is about to get a lot better, right before it becomes unnecessary.
